title: Ancient Snake with Legs Found!
summary: Scientists found a very old snake fossil in Argentina. This snake lived almost 100 million years ago and had small back legs! Today's snakes don't have legs. The fossil also had a cheekbone that most snakes today don't have. This helps scientists learn how snakes changed over time. Long ago, people thought the first snakes were small and lived underground. But this fossil shows that early snakes were big and had big mouths. They kept their legs for a long time before losing them. Scientists used special X-rays to see inside the fossil without breaking it. They could see bones and even nerves inside the skull. This discovery helps us understand how snakes evolved.

keywords.explanation: the remains of a plant or animal from long ago that turned into stone
to change slowly over many, many years
the bones that make up the head
a bone in the face near the cheek

questions.question: How old is the snake fossil?
What special feature did this ancient snake have?
What bone did this snake have that most snakes today don't have?
How did scientists see inside the fossil?
What did scientists think early snakes were like before this fossil?

background_read: Snakes are reptiles that have no legs and move by slithering. But long ago, some snakes had small legs. Fossils help us learn about animals that lived millions of years ago. Scientists study fossils to understand how animals changed over time.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists found a snake fossil in Argentina.
WHAT: The fossil shows an ancient snake with back legs and a cheekbone.
WHERE: The fossil was found in Argentina, in a place called Patagonia.
WHY: The discovery helps scientists understand how snakes evolved from animals with legs to animals without legs.

perspectives.description: They are excited because the fossil gives new information about snake evolution.
They can learn how animals change over time and how scientists study the past.
